stories.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports. And, Orbán loses Hungarian election
stories.description: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

stories.title: Trump vows to sink Iranian ships approaching a U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: President Trump said the U.S. would interdict vessels that had to pay what he called an "illegal toll" to Iran to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports after peace talks fail
stories.description: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.

stories.title: Retired U.S. Navy admiral on Trump's threat to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to retired U.S. Navy Adm. James Foggo, dean of the Center for Maritime Strategy, about President Trump's command to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism amid growing Vatican–U.S. tensions over Iran war
stories.description: Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: President Trump says U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz, failed U.S.-Iran negotiations cause oil prices to surge, Hungary's Viktor Orbán loses reelection bid.

stories.title: Oil prices surge after U.S.-Iran peace talks break down
stories.description: The U.S. said on Sunday that it will soon block ships from Iranian ports. It comes after the U.S. and Iran failed to reach an agreement during face-to-face peace talks, sending oil prices surging.

stories.title: Former U.S. special envoy on where U.S. and Iran stand after failed negotiations
stories.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Robert Malley, former U.S. special envoy for Iran, about where the weekend's negotiations between the U.S. and Iran leave diplomatic efforts to resolve the war.

stories.title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
stories.description: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

stories.title: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.description: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
stories.description: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

stories.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
stories.description: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

stories.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
stories.description: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

stories.title: Lebanon and Israel officials to meet in US on Tuesday
stories.description: A senior Lebanese official earlier told the BBC that Lebanon would only take part in talks if a ceasefire was in place.

stories.title: Historic Vance-Ghalibaf talks must bridge deep distrust
stories.description: Face-to-face talks would mark the highest-level engagement between Iran and the US since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.

stories.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.description: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
stories.description: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

stories.title: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: The first vessel has crossed the Strait of Hormuz after the US imposed a military blockade of Iran's ports.

stories.title: Deadline passes for US blockade of Hormuz, Iran threatens to retaliate against Gulf neighbours
stories.description: The deadline passed on Monday for the start of a US ​military blockade of ships leaving Iran's ports, and Tehran threatened to retaliate against ports of its Gulf neighbours after weekend talks on ending the war broke down. Oil prices surged, with no sign of a swift reopening of ...

stories.title: US-Vatican feud over Iran: Pope Leo pushes back on Trump's criticism
stories.description: The Pope pushed back Monday on President Donald Trump’s criticism of him over the US-Israel war in Iran, telling reporters that the Vatican’s appeals for peace and reconciliation are rooted in the Gospel, and that he doesn’t fear the Trump administration. Leo XIV's comments came ...

stories.title: US says to blockade Iran ports: What could be the consequences?
stories.description: The US military said it would begin a blockade of all Iranian ports Monday, after talks between the warring sides in Pakistan collapsed, and despite the Iranian military warning that it would treat any such action as an act of piracy. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor take...

stories.title: Israel presses assault on Lebanon border town ahead of US-hosted talks
stories.description: Israeli troops launched an attack on Monday to seize a key town in south Lebanon from Hezbollah fighters holed up there, pressing the war on the Iran-backed group on the eve of rare talks between Israeli and Lebanese government envoys. With the Lebanese and Israeli ambassadors to...

stories.title: EU: No peace possible while “Lebanon is in flames”
stories.description: Von der Leyen warns Hormuz closure and Israeli strikes on Lebanon threaten regional stability.

stories.title: What are the pros and cons of Trump’s Iranian naval blockade?
stories.description: What does Donald Trump’s naval blockade of Iranian ports look like and can it achieve what the US president wants?

stories.title: Bahrain summons Iraqi envoy as pro-Iranian attacks persist in Gulf
stories.description: Move reflects regional alarm over attacks by pro-Iranian groups based in Iraq, which continue despite ceasefire.

stories.title: Iran releases assets of women’s football captain in Australia asylum row
stories.description: Zahra Ghanbari, one of the six players who sought asylum in Australia, reversed her decision and returned to Iran.

stories.title: Lebanon Latest: Talks scheduled as Israeli strikes continue
stories.description: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon push death toll past 2,000, as talks loom and ceasefire divisions deepen.

stories.title: What do we know about sea mines in and around the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: The psychological terror that the presence of sea mines can generate could paralyse the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump says Iranian ships to be ‘eliminated’ as US naval blockade begins
stories.description: Iran describes the siege against its ports as the US president's 'revenge of choice' against the global economy.

stories.title: Trump’s threat to blockade Hormuz: Why it’s the latest major escalation
stories.description: Analysts warn Washington's blockade could harm the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Luxury brands face profit squeeze as Iran conflict shrinks UAE mall sales, sources say
stories.description: Sales at Europe’s biggest luxury brands have shrunk in Dubai and Abu Dhabi as the Iran conflict hit the sector’s fastest-growing market in the latest setback for the $400 billion industry whose value has contracted over the last three years. Luxury brands in March reported sales ...

stories.title: US military to begin blockade of Iranian ports on Monday
stories.description: The US military said it would begin a blockade of all Iranian ports on Monday, after talks between the warring sides in Pakistan failed to produce an agreement, with President Donald Trump blaming the Islamic republic’s refusal to abandon its nuclear ambitions. Trump had announce...

stories.title: Iran ‘will not bow to any threats’, Ghalibaf declares
stories.description: • Iran parliament speaker says US must decide ‘whether it can earn our trust or not’• Tehran warns of strong response to any confrontation; calls control over strait ‘non-negotiable’• Iran demands full sanction lifting; US offers phased approach• Conflicting claims over $6bn froz...

stories.title: Pope sees ‘moral obligation’ to protect Lebanese
stories.description: Pope Leo XIV VATICAN CITY: Pope Leo XIV expressed his closeness to the people of Lebanon on Sunday, saying there was a “moral obligation” to protect them while calling on warring parties to seek peace. Lebanese authorities say more than 2,000 people have been killed in Israeli st...

stories.title: After Trump’s blockade announcement, IRGC says will view military vessels approaching Hormuz as ceasefire breach
stories.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) said on Sunday that any military vessels attempting to approach the Strait of Hormuz would be considered a violation of the two-week ceasefire with the US, and warned that it would be dealt with harshly and decisively. “The strait ...

stories.title: Dar urges US, Iran to uphold commitment to ceasefire as talks conclude without deal
stories.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar urged the United States and Iran to remain committed to a ceasefire after both countries ended historic face-to-face talks without an agreement on Sunday. “It is imperative that the parties continue to uphold their commitment t...

stories.title: Russia offers to take in Iran's enriched uranium
stories.description: Failed talks between Iran and the United States over the weekend dashed hopes of a swift deal to permanently end the war that has killed thousands and thrown the global economy into turmoil since it began in late February

stories.title: Iran executed at least 1,639 in 2025, more hangings feared: NGOs
stories.description: The number of executions represented an increase of 6% on the 975 people Iran put to death in 2024, and also included 48 women, Norway-based Iran Human Rights (IHR) and Paris-based Together Against the Death Penalty (ECPM) said in their joint annual report

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE updates: Donald Trump warns Iran boats breaching U.S. blockade will be ‘eliminated’
stories.description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said a ceasefire between the United States and Iran was “holding” and that efforts were under way to reach an agreement

stories.title: U.S., Israel met ‘strategic defeat’ in Iran, says Ambassador Fathali
stories.description: The Envoy said the U.S. side made "unlawful" demands during the talks that led to the negotiations ending without results

stories.title: Donald Trump vows U.S. will sink any Iran boats that challenge blockade
stories.description: The U.S. military said the blockade would begin at 1930 hours IST and apply to all ships leaving or seeking to dock at Iranian harbours, but as the deadline passed there was no announcement that the measure had come into effect, nor sign of any interceptions

stories.title: U.K. will not back blockade of Strait of Hormuz, says PM Starmer
stories.description: Britain had minesweepers in the ​region, Starmer said, and ‌while he could not discuss operational matters, the military capability was “focused from our point of view on getting the Strait full open”
